seo.title: AI Agent Error Handling Patterns in BeeAI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent error handling patterns in BeeAI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In BeeAI, AI Agent Error Handling Patterns manifest as critical reliability gaps during long-running agent sessions, where unhandled exceptions in tool calls, memory overflows, and model output parsing failures cascade into agent crashes or silent data corruption. BeeAI's event-driven architecture amplifies these issues, as agents continue executing despite tool validation failures and serialization errors in state persistence. Without robust error boundaries, BeeAI agents exhibit unpredictable behavior in production, particularly with Granite model's inconsistent JSON outputs.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent memory growing unbounded in long sessions
howItManifests.symptom: AgentContext.memory_size exceeds 100MB after 30+ tool calls, OOM errors in worker processes
howItManifests.impact: Agent crashes mid-conversation, loses entire session state, 40% session abandonment rate

howItManifests.symptom: AgentTool.call() accepts malformed inputs like 'rm -rf /', executes without sanitization
howItManifests.impact: Security vulnerabilities, data leaks, arbitrary code execution in sandboxed environments

howItManifests.scenario: Serialization errors in agent state persistence
howItManifests.symptom: AgentState.save() fails with JSONDecodeError on circular references in tool outputs
howItManifests.impact: Lost conversation history, inconsistent agent behavior across restarts

howItManifests.symptom: parse_granite_response() fails with KeyError: 'tool_calls' missing from LLM output
howItManifests.impact: Agent loops indefinitely waiting for valid tool calls, 200% increased latency

detection.name: Memory Growth Monitor
detection.description: Detects unbounded memory growth by tracking AgentContext memory usage over time

detection.name: Tool Injection Detector
detection.description: Scans AgentTool.call() inputs for dangerous shell commands and injection patterns

detection.name: Granite Parse Failure Tracker
detection.description: Monitors parse_granite_response() exceptions and format inconsistencies

fixes.name: Memory Truncation Middleware
fixes.description: Automatically truncates AgentContext memory when exceeding 50MB threshold

fixes.name: Secure Tool Validator
fixes.description: Input sanitization wrapper for all AgentTool implementations

fixes.name: Robust Granite Parser
fixes.description: Graceful fallback parser for Granite model inconsistencies

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Error Handling Patterns Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly with BeeAI's event system as a drop-in event listener, automatically detecting all four error patterns across your agent fleet. By registering VexAgentObserver on Agent.run() events, it captures MemoryEvent, ToolCallEvent, and LLMResponseEvent streams in real-time, applying the detection logic above without modifying agent code. Vex provides instant alerting, automated remediation via middleware injection, and comprehensive dashboards showing error rates by agent, tool, and session duration.

The Vex SDK auto-instruments BeeAI agents during initialization, requiring only one line of setup code. It handles rate-limiting, deduplication, and severity prioritization automatically, while providing remediation APIs that inject fixes like MemoryLimiterMiddleware directly into failing agents. Vex's ML-powered anomaly detection learns your baseline patterns, reducing false positives over time and suggesting optimal thresholds for your specific workloads.
vexAutomation.code:
from beeai import Agent
from vex_sdk import VexAgentObserver

# Single line integration
agent = Agent(name='production-agent')
agent.add_observer(VexAgentObserver())

# Vex auto-detects these patterns:
# ✅ Unbounded memory growth
# ✅ Tool injection attempts
# ✅ Serialization failures
# ✅ Granite parse errors

agent.run()  # Vex handles everything automatically

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex in 30 seconds and eliminate BeeAI error handling issues across your entire agent fleet: pip install vex-sdk
